titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Great Britain ( (Bingham))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in Great Britain ( Bingham )
div.p: 253. [For Davis.] Your 335, June 19, 5 p.m. We fully concur with the emphasis you lay on the necessity of keeping the present naval conversations most secret. PhillipsWilliam Phillips, Under Secretary of State. and MoffatPierrepont Moffat, Chief of the Division of Western European Affairs. have talked with Admiral StandleyWilliam H. Standley, Chief of Naval Operations. and have worked out a system which it is believed will prevent any possible leaks.

We are playing down the conversations here telling the press that we anticipate they will be rather drawn out and undramatic. There has been a good deal of publicity from London which we are watching closely. Is it not possible that the correspondents have some of our naval views prepared for the 1932 Geneva ConferenceConference for the Reduction and Limitation of Armaments; for correspondence, see Foreign Relations, 1932, vol. i, pp. 1 ff. and are endeavoring to smoke out your position by pretending to have information of our present position? At any rate, you can count on the utmost care and cooperation at this end.
